question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with each of the following words:

Harness, Landfill, Conserve, Algae, Geothermal
solution_html:
Harness: Wind turbines harness natural energy.

Landfill: Every second of every day, a garbage-truck-sized load of clothing is dumped in landfills.

Conserve: We must conserve water during dry seasons.

Algae: Nutrient overload leads to harmful algae blooms.

Geothermal: Geothermal energy taps into earth’s internal heat.

Marks Distribution:

For making meaningful correct sentences; (0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.]

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with appropriate words:

The train rattled gently along the tracks, windows (a) ____ scenes of golden fields and sleepy towns. Inside, passengers sat in (b) ____ contemplation, or murmured stories to (c) ____ strangers. The rhythmic motion and distant whistle created a (d) ____ lullaby. It was a journey filled with motion and (e) ____.
solution_html:
(a) framing / capturing / reflecting / revealing / displaying / showing / portraying / presenting

(b) quiet / silent / peaceful / gentle / serene / tranquil / meditative

(c) curious / friendly / eager / approachable / welcoming / cordial

(d) soothing / gentle / soft / comforting / calming / melodic

(e) thought / memory / solitude / reflection / reverie / introspection

Marks Distribution:

For filling gaps with appropriate words; (0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks.

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Street Children of Bangladesh’.
solution_html:
Street Children of Bangladesh

Amid the chaos of city traffic and the noise of busy streets, a silent crisis continues to grow each day. This crisis is the harsh and heartbreaking reality of street children. In Bangladesh, thousands of children live without shelter, family care, or protection. Many are victims of poverty, domestic violence, broken families, or natural disasters. They migrate from rural areas to cities in search of food and survival. Most are found working as beggars, helpers, vendors, or waste collectors. Some are addicted to cheap harmful substances such as dandy or glue, which offer a brief escape from their painful reality. These children suffer from hunger, abuse, disease, and total neglect. They are denied education, medical care, and the chance of a better life. Although a few organizations provide limited support, their reach is not enough. These children are not at fault; they are the result of a failing system. Giving them love, safety, and opportunity is our moral and national duty.

Mark distribution:

For writing Title ‘Street Children of Bangladesh’

; 0.5 mark.

For writing clear explanation/definition of street children of Bangladesh; 02 marks.

For writing key points about street children of Bangladesh; 02 marks.

For writing final thought about street children of Bangladesh; 0.5 mark.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.
